title: Domain Records, Pure Pipeline Stages, and Serialization · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for domain records, pure pipeline stages, and serialization and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, terminal, packaging, operating-system, load, security, and release transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for typed cumulative analysis pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.
Use immutable records where post-computation mutation would invalidate retained evidence.
Give each pipeline stage typed input, output, invariant, failure, and version contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Serialization Schema
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Serialization Schema: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Bookbot team handles case 5065 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose typed stages and immutable evidence under a versioned serialization schema; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Version JSON or CSV report schemas, preserve types and ordering, and reject non-finite or ambiguous values.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Serialization Schema.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Serialization Schema, keep Serialization Schema connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Version JSON or CSV report schemas, preserve types and ordering, and reject non-finite or ambiguous values.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Serialization Schema, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Serialization Schema precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Serialization Schema, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Bookbot team handles case 5065 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose typed stages and immutable evidence under a versioned serialization schema; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Version JSON or CSV report schemas, preserve types and ordering, and reject non-finite or ambiguous values.

For Bookbot and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Serialization Schema, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Python repr output is a stable cross-language data format.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Domain Records to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Domain Records.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using version and json plus specific case evidence.
